title: Safety Instrumented System Monitoring
app: SIS vendor DCS export, OPC-UA alarms
spl:
index=ot sourcetype="sis:event"
| where event_type IN ("trip","bypass","fault","demand")
| table _time, sis_tag, event_type, cause, sil_level
| sort -_time
description: SIS trips, bypasses, and diagnostics demand immediate visibility and audit trails.
value: SIS trips, bypasses, and diagnostics demand immediate visibility and audit trails.
implementation: Segregate SIS data per safety policy. Never route writes from IT networks. Alert on any bypass or fault.
visualization: Timeline (SIS events), Single value (active bypasses), Table (trip history).
knownFalsePositives: Similar activity during production startups, control downloads, and approved remote maintenance—triage with the SCADA HMI and change log.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: SIS vendor DCS export, OPC-UA alarms.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="sis:event"` (trip, bypass, fault).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Segregate SIS data per safety policy. Never route writes from IT networks. Alert on any bypass or fault.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="sis:event"
| where event_type IN ("trip","bypass","fault","demand")
| table _time, sis_tag, event_type, cause, sil_level
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Safety Instrumented System Monitoring** — SIS trips, bypasses, and diagnostics demand immediate visibility and audit trails.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="sis:event"` (trip, bypass, fault). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): SIS vendor DCS export, OPC-UA alarms. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: sis:event. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="sis:event". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where event_type IN ("trip","bypass","fault","demand")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Safety Instrumented System Monitoring**): table _time, sis_tag, event_type, cause, sil_level
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search when the process is in a known state. Compare key fields and alarm states to the SCADA or DCS HMI and, where relevant, the engineering tool for the PLC. Confirm sourcetypes and field extractions match the vendor feed.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (SIS events), Single value (active bypasses), Table (trip history).
grandmaExplanation: We watch your plant control systems and field data. We help you know if something is off with this area: safety instrumented system before harm, damage, or wasted effort piles up.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable (no CIM or Operational_Telemetry data model in this SPL).
